summary: Closeout handoff plan, function-budget owner backlog, and release-gate taxonomy split for the 2026-05-11 runtime maintenance slice.

observations.problem: The post-check finalizer artifact identified recommended targets, but operators still had to infer why each target mattered, which files it would write, and whether the attention state meant missing evidence or real drift.
observations.why_it_matters: Closeout failures need a bounded handoff artifact that can be uploaded from CI and executed deliberately instead of treating a broad attention report as an implicit runbook.
observations.automation_candidate: Generate a plan JSON beside the dry-run report and target text, with target-level why/writes/expected digest/safe dry-run fields and a status that separates evidence insufficiency from drift.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated in this task: release-closeout-post-check-finalizer-ci-artifact now writes tmp/release-closeout-post-check-finalizer-plan.json, uploads it from CI/release workflows, and the report exposes diagnostic_status values settled, evidence_insufficient, drift_detected, and mixed_attention.

observations.problem: After small proposals reached zero, the remaining function-budget candidates were broad owner surfaces; a flat non-proposal count encouraged metric chasing instead of design backlog handling.
observations.why_it_matters: Large report builders and shared runtime helpers should be managed by owner sequencing and direct tests, while low-risk parameter budget work should use the established request-object pattern.
observations.automation_candidate: Promote non-proposal candidates into owner_backlog[] and handle one owner surface at a time, starting with direct tests for external_report_reference_manifest.build_report.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated in this task: function-budget reports now include owner_backlog[]; external_report_reference_manifest.build_report now takes ExternalReportReferenceManifestRequest/ZipIdentityInput; regenerated function-budget evidence reports proposal_count=0, non_proposal_candidate_count=41, owner_backlog_count=14, and no external_report_reference_manifest.build_report candidate.

observations.problem: Learning readiness vocabulary allowed downstream release-gate promotion blocker IDs that release-risk-taxonomy did not classify, so release closeout mixed real authority blockers with taxonomy coverage drift and could self-block the source closeout lane.
observations.why_it_matters: Release authority handoff needs to distinguish source clean-lane evidence from downstream sealing/finality gates; otherwise operators see an unregistered-code blocker or a circular closeout-summary blocker instead of the actual sealed-release state.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep learning release blocker IDs registered in release-risk-taxonomy and test that downstream release authority promotion blockers do not block source closeout clean lane while still blocking learning/promotion claims.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated in this task: release-risk-taxonomy now registers all learning release blocker IDs; downstream release closeout, batch manifest, and finality promotion blockers are classified as does_not_block_clean_lane/operator_review_required/blocks_learning_claim; tests lock vocabulary-taxonomy coverage and source closeout self-block behavior.
